issues.title: Mention user fingerprinting security considerations
issues.author: ounsworth
issues.body:
> When considering caching large public keys for TLS (or other protocols), please make sure the security considerations section carefully considers whether the proposed mechanism leaks information about whether the client has previously contacted the server and possibly how recently, etc.  

> -Tim Hollebeek




https://mailarchive.ietf.org/arch/msg/tls/DQSFbz2MHvyLnxC9OzR7GTJ8n94/

issues.title: We should specify the encoding of the hashed public key
issues.author: ounsworth
issues.body:
Currently our draft leaves unspecified what the format is of the hashed public key.

As an example to follow, we should probably clone Russ' Hash Of Root Key RFC 8649 which specifies it as "Hash of DER-encoded SubjectPublicKeyInfo". 

We should do the same.

https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc8649#section-3

```
   HashedRootKey ::= SEQUENCE {
      hashAlg        HashAlgorithm,        -- Hash algorithm used
      hashValue      OCTET STRING }        -- Hash of DER-encoded
                                           --   SubjectPublicKeyInfo
```

comments.author: CBonnell
comments.body:
Turns out that Section 2.1 says:
"When ExternalValue is placed into a SubjectPublicKeyInfo.subjectPublicKey, the ExternalValue.location MUST refer to a DER-encoded SubjectPublicKeyInfo, which MAY be base64 encoded as per [[RFC4648](https://www.ietf.org/archive/id/draft-ounsworth-lamps-pq-external-pubkeys-02.html#RFC4648)] for easier transport over text protocols."

So DER encoding is already specified. However, I'm unsure how base64 encoding is signaled. It might be best to leave that to the transport protocol (HTTP, etc.).

issues.title: We should specify which URI schemes are allowed
issues.author: ounsworth
issues.body:
Following the examples in 5280, we should say something like:

>    Conforming implementations MUST recognize and perform scheme-based
   normalization for the following schemes: ldap, http, https, and ftp.

pulls.title: Wrote David's idea of GeneralNames instead of GeneralName
pulls.author: ounsworth

comments.author: johngray-dev
comments.body: I support the addition of GeneralNames into the ASN.1 of this keytype.   Thanks for adding the pull request!
